message:
fix(decoder,linalg): bounds guards, pivot-column exclusion, and factor=1 fast path in elimination core

Harden the GF(256) Gaussian elimination pipeline against edge cases
and improve performance for the common unity-factor case:

linalg.rs:
- Guard first_nonzero_from and row_first_nonzero_from against
  start >= row.len() to prevent panics on out-of-bounds access
- Use get().copied().unwrap_or(0) in select_pivot_basic/markowitz
  for short-row safety instead of direct indexing
- Cap Markowitz nnz scan at best_nnz-1 since ties cannot improve
  the incumbent (first winner stays by row-order tiebreak)

decoder.rs:
- New sparse_update_columns_for_elimination_if_beneficial_into
  removes the pivot column before the sparse elimination loop,
  replacing the fragile in-loop `continue` guard with an explicit
  precondition enforced by debug_assert
- New eliminate_row_rhs dispatches to gf256_add_slice when
  factor==ONE, avoiding the multiply in the common unity case
- factor_is_one fast paths in eliminate_row_coefficients for both
  sparse and dense modes
- Tests: sparse column pivot exclusion, RHS helper for both factor
  paths, extended elimination tests parameterized over factor=ONE
  and factor=nonone

Resolves the gf256_addmul_slice compile-mismatch that was blocking
E5 closure runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
